sales.title:
Ancients
TROAS. Alexandria. Maximus, as Caesar (AD 235/6-238). AE (25mm, 8.65 gm, 5h). NGC VF S 5/5 - 4/5. IVL MAX-IMVS CAE, bare headed, draped, and cuirassed bust of Maximus right, seen from behind / COL-TR-O, Nike standing facing on globe, shield in both hands over head, on arch with three vaults; trophies to left and right. RPC VI Online Temporary 4060 (this specimen). A exemplary example of this type with rich mahogany patina. Ex Solidus Numismatik, Auction 138 (10 December 2024), lot 1180. Son of Maximinus I and Caecilia Paulina, Gaius Julius Verus Maximus was appointed Caesar by his father ca. AD 236. However, his reign was short lived as both he and his father were murdered two years later by the Praetorian Guard during the Siege of Aquileia. His death gave way for Pupienus and Balbinus to become co-emperors. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice
